description: Surgical and procedural interventions available during the appendicitis encounter. Accessible via Icon 8 (Surgeries & Procedures) and Icon 10 (Airway).

procedures.name: Intubation (RSI)
procedures.description: Endotracheal intubation via rapid sequence intubation protocol. Player must give sedative and paralytic via medication browser before confirming.

rsi_checklist.description: MEDDY presents a safety checklist before intubation is confirmed. Player checks boxes manually. MEDDY does not verify — engine checks reality after confirmation.

items.label: Equipment ready (bag-mask, laryngoscope, ET tube, stylet)

rsi_checklist.note: Checklist is for player awareness only. Engine independently verifies sedative and paralytic were actually ordered via medication browser.

failure_ongoing.note: Giving sedation after intubation stops further accrual immediately. All previously accrued harm points remain.

checks.meddy_feedback_failure: Oh no — the patient wasn't sedated before intubation! They are awake and aware. This is causing serious harm. Give a sedative NOW to stop further damage!
No paralytic was given before intubation. The patient wasn't fully relaxed — this caused harm. Always give a paralytic before intubating!
Intubating this patient right now caused harm — they didn't need their airway secured yet. Intubation is appropriate in septic shock with respiratory compromise, not before.
checks.meddy_feedback_pass: Good call securing the airway in septic shock. Intubation is appropriate here.

engine_verification.note: All checks are independent. Harm points from each failed check stack.

on_success.meddy_feedback: Intubation successful. Patient is on the ventilator. Check your settings and keep an eye on those vitals.

procedures.name: Appendectomy
procedures.description: Surgical removal of the appendix. This is the definitive treatment and win condition for appendicitis.

prerequisites.note: At least one imaging study OR a general surgery consult must be completed before appendectomy is available.
prerequisites.missing_prerequisite_popup: You'll need imaging or a surgery consult before heading to the OR. Talk to General Surgery or get a CT first.

stable.meddy_feedback: Off to the OR! The appendix is coming out. Great work getting here efficiently.

perforated.meddy_feedback: The appendix has already perforated, but we're getting it out now. Expect a longer recovery, but this was the right call.

septic_shock.resuscitation_definition: IV fluids and at least one vasopressor ordered
septic_shock.without_resuscitation_popup: The patient is in septic shock — going straight to the OR without resuscitation first is dangerous. Stabilize with fluids and vasopressors first.
septic_shock.meddy_feedback_resuscitated: Good — you stabilized the patient before the OR. This is the right approach in septic shock.
septic_shock.meddy_feedback_not_resuscitated: Taking a septic shock patient to the OR without resuscitation caused harm. Always stabilize first!

coma_fail.popup: It's too late. The patient has deteriorated beyond the point where surgery can help.

on_success.meddy_feedback_stable: Appendectomy complete! The patient is going to make a full recovery. Excellent work, Doctor!
on_success.meddy_feedback_perforated: Appendectomy complete. It was a tough case — the appendix had already ruptured — but you got there. Well done.
on_success.meddy_feedback_septic_shock: Appendectomy complete. This was a critically ill patient and you pulled it off. Outstanding work.

procedures.name: Central Line Placement
procedures.description: Placement of a central venous catheter. Useful in septic shock for vasopressor administration and monitoring.

effects.clinical_note: Appropriate in septic shock. Required for certain vasopressors in some protocols. No direct effect on appendicitis progression.

procedures.name: Arterial Line Placement
procedures.description: Placement of an arterial catheter for continuous beat-to-beat blood pressure monitoring and frequent blood draws.

effects.clinical_note: Appropriate in septic shock. Enables continuous BP monitoring and cheap ABG draws.

unlocks.description: ABG now costs 1 AP instead of 6 AP for the remainder of the encounter.
unlocks.meddy_feedback: Arterial line is in! ABG draws are now quick and easy — only 1 AP from here on.

new_vital_sign.name: Continuous Arterial Pressure
new_vital_sign.description: Beat-to-beat arterial pressure tracing replaces intermittent cuff BP on the monitor. Shows characteristic waveform shape, amplitude, and heart rate.

state_appearances.stable: Normal amplitude waveform, regular rate ~105 bpm
state_appearances.septic_shock: Low amplitude, wide pulse pressure, tachycardic ~135 bpm — classic septic shock pattern

effects.clinical_note: No indication in appendicitis. No effect. Costs AP.
No primary indication in appendicitis. No significant effect. Costs AP.
No indication in appendicitis. No effect. Costs AP.

note: No bonus points available from surgeries and procedures in the appendicitis case.
